message:
fix(pi-agent): always emit content as array to prevent TypeError in Pi (#9)

Pi's JS runtime calls `message.content.some(...)` on every message, which
is an Array method. The writer was emitting `content` as a plain string for
messages with no tool calls, causing `TypeError: message.content.some is not
a function` on session resume after any Codex→Pi conversion.

Fix: always build content as `[{type:"text", text:"..."}]` regardless of
whether tool calls are present. The reader already handles both formats via
`flatten_content`, so round-trip is unaffected.

Also fixes the same pattern in the test `write_and_read_back` helper and
adds a regression test that asserts no written message entry has plain-string
content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
